title: Chronic Hip Pain: Why I Didn't Need the Surgery They Recommended
subtitle: How a systematic, gait-respecting approach resolved hip pain that Copenhagens and clamshells couldn't.
excerpt: A surgeon told me I'd probably need surgery. The standard rehab — Copenhagens, clamshells, pistol squats — wasn't moving the needle. Here's why those exercises miss the point of hip mechanics, and the systematic approach that actually worked.
imageAlt: Side view of a runner in mid-stance — the gait position where hip mechanics actually matter

title: The First Four: Movements to Prioritise
subtitle: Standing, walking, running & throwing — the four pillars of every human day.
excerpt: Almost everything you do reduces to four patterns. When they're well-organised, your day has less friction. When they're compensated, it shows up as pain. Here's what each one is — and how precise strength training rebuilds them.
imageAlt: Usain Bolt sprinting at the 2008 Beijing Olympics
